visual_inventory.subject: Razeen Shaheed — faux.thinker AI avatar
visual_inventory.framing: medium-tight, head + torso, direct-to-camera, subject fills ~60% of canvas height
visual_inventory.wardrobe: black pullover hoodie with drawstrings (classic faux.thinker wardrobe)
visual_inventory.backdrop: warm-LED plant shelf, trailing pothos, hexagonal wall panels, speaker/shelf assembly, chair back visible lower-left
visual_inventory.lighting: warm 2800-3200K keylight from above-right + rim from shelf LED + soft fill, no harsh shadows
visual_inventory.color_grade: warm natural, slightly lifted blacks, no stylized LUT
visual_inventory.camera_motion: static — no push-in, no handheld, no rack focus
visual_inventory.gesture: minimal hand-visible — shoulders-up framing, most energy is facial/vocal
visual_inventory.audio_room: dry, clean, consistent — likely de-noised + EQ'd VO, no room tone bleed

beats.vo: Anthropic dropped a new Claude model three hours ago. It is the second best model they have ever built and most people are going to miss what actually matters.
Here is everything you need in 60 seconds.

beats.function: the product name lands — candidate for EmphasisCaption

beats.vo: The best model Mythos is still locked away. Anthropic is testing safety stuff on 4.7 first.
beats.function: hidden-information frame — candidate for FactoidCard + EmphasisCaption 'MYTHOS'

beats.vo: It is a real jump. Claude's own benchmark went from 58% to 70%.
beats.function: quantified proof — candidate for RevenueChart or StatCounter (58 → 70)

beats.vo: Rakuten says it solves 3x more production tasks than the last version.
This is not a small update. Your old prompts might break.
beats.function: stakes raise — candidate for EmphasisCaption 'NOT A SMALL UPDATE'

beats.vo: 4.7 follows instructions literally now. The lazy prompts you wrote six months ago, retune them or get weird results.
beats.function: behavior change detail — candidate for B-roll + EmphasisCaption 'LITERALLY'

beats.vo: Your API bill is going up. New tokenizer, more thinking, more output tokens. Cost control is on you now.
beats.function: cost warning — candidate for FactoidCard + EmphasisCaption 'YOUR BILL IS GOING UP'

beats.vo: And here is the part nobody is talking about. Low effort 4.7 is basically the same quality as medium effort 4.6. Meaning you can get better work for less money if you know which setting to use.
beats.function: the actual payoff — EmphasisCaption 'LESS MONEY' or 'NOBODY IS TALKING ABOUT'

beats.vo: I am rebuilding three of my client automations on this today. Comment the word Opus and I will send you my updated prompt template for 4.7.

known_word_mishears_to_fix.right: (confirm spelling — possibly 'Mithos' or a code-name the user invented)

style_summary.concept: short-form AI-news announcement reel — Claude Opus 4.7 release + hidden-insight angle + DM-gated prompt template CTA

alignment_with_faux_thinker_template.target_length: 25-45 s (style guide §2) — current video is 79 s; either extend target or trim script
alignment_with_faux_thinker_template.profile_card_anchor: creator name is NOT spoken in this script — ProfileCard will need an alternate anchor beat (e.g. Opus logo card or skip entirely)
alignment_with_faux_thinker_template.revenue_chart_candidate: beat 5 (58 → 70) maps well to RevenueChart-style animated stat reveal
b_roll_candidates: API pricing page for 'bill going up' beat
alignment_with_faux_thinker_template.emphasis_caption_candidates: multiple candidates (MYTHOS / NOT A SMALL UPDATE / LITERALLY / LESS MONEY) — style guide says emphasis caption stands alone; need to pick 1–2
alignment_with_faux_thinker_template.dm_toast_cta: beat 11 — trigger word 'Opus', payload 'updated prompt template for 4.7'
